dashboard_recap.headline: MLB Daily Recap - May 15, 2026
dashboard_recap.article:
**Phillies Outlast Pirates in Thrilling 11-9 Victory**

Kyle Schwarber's monstrous performance led the Philadelphia Phillies to an 11-9 comeback win over the Pittsburgh Pirates. Schwarber crushed two home runs and drove in five runs, while Bryce Harper added three hits and an RBI. The Phillies' bullpen struggled to hold a lead, but José Alvarado shut the door with a scoreless inning, earning the win. Alvarado struck out three batters in the process.

In other action, the Washington Nationals edged the Baltimore Orioles 3-2 behind Daylen Lile's three-hit performance, including a home run and two RBIs. Zack Littell pitched five scoreless innings for the Nationals. The Detroit Tigers took down the Toronto Blue Jays 3-2, thanks in part to Trey Yesavage's six-inning outing, in which he struck out six batters.

The Cincinnati Reds held off the Cleveland Guardians 7-6, with Matt McLain's two-hit, three-RBI performance leading the way. Andrew Abbott earned the win, while Pierce Johnson struck out two batters in a scoreless inning.
